use tree_sitter::Parser;
use std::collections::HashMap;

fn parse_files(files: &[&str]) {
    let mut languages: HashMap<String, tree_sitter::Language> = HashMap::new();

    for file_path in files {
        let source = std::fs::read_to_string(file_path).unwrap();
        let ext = file_path.rsplit('.').next().unwrap_or("");
        let language = languages
            .entry(ext.to_string())
            .or_insert_with(|| get_language_for_ext(ext));

        let mut parser = Parser::new();
        parser.set_language(language).unwrap();
        let tree = parser.parse(&source, None).unwrap();
        process_tree(tree.root_node(), &source);
    }
}

fn get_language_for_ext(ext: &str) -> tree_sitter::Language {
    match ext {
        "rs" => tree_sitter_rust::LANGUAGE.into(),
        "py" => tree_sitter_python::LANGUAGE.into(),
        _ => tree_sitter_rust::LANGUAGE.into(),
    }
}

fn process_tree(_node: tree_sitter::Node, _source: &str) {}
